unit.name: Rotation prediction for visual commonsense
unit.description: Introduces rotation prediction as a concrete pretext task, explains why it may induce holistic image understanding, and reviews empirical transfer results.
unit.summary: Rotation prediction is introduced as a pretext task based on the hypothesis that a model can identify the correct orientation of an object only if it has learned visual commonsense about how the object should normally appear [ts=956s] [ts=974s]. The specific example uses four discrete rotations—0, 90, 180, and 270 degrees—so the task becomes 4-way classification rather than direct angle regression [ts=1004s] [ts=1048s]. The lecture then describes transfer results: on CIFAR-10, a rotation-pretrained model starts from a stronger initial accuracy and can match supervised training on an easy task, while on harder downstream tasks and Pascal VOC benchmarks it substantially outperforms random initialization and approaches fully supervised ImageNet pretraining [ts=1092s] [ts=1284s]. The segment ends by noting that self-supervised attention maps often cover more of the object than supervised ones because the representation must remain useful across unknown downstream tasks [ts=1332s] [ts=1375s].

key_points.text: Rotation prediction is motivated by the idea that correct orientation recognition requires visual commonsense about unperturbed objects.
Using the set {0, 90, 180, 270} turns rotation prediction into a 4-class classification problem.
In the CIFAR-10 experiment, conv1 and conv2 are frozen while later layers and a linear head are adapted with labeled data.
Rotation pretraining is much better than random initialization and gets close to supervised ImageNet pretraining on Pascal VOC transfer tasks.
Self-supervised attention maps are often more holistic than supervised ones because the representation must support multiple possible downstream tasks.

kp_catalog.name: Rotation prediction
kp_catalog.description: A pretext task that predicts discrete image rotations to encourage representations with visual commonsense about object orientation.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because rotation prediction clarifies a pretext task that predicts discrete image rotations to encourage representations with visual commonsense about object orientation, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Downstream transfer and extrinsic evaluation
kp_catalog.description: Self-supervised methods should be judged mainly by how useful their learned representations are for downstream tasks, often via linear evaluation or fine-tuning. A pre-trained encoder can be reused for downstream tasks by adding lightweight supervised heads such as linear classifiers or decoders.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because downstream transfer and extrinsic evaluation clarifies self-supervised methods should be judged mainly by how useful their learned representations are for downstream tasks, often via linear evaluation or fine-tuning, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.
